free_text: Mass distributions of fission fragments from nuclei
populated by multinucleon transfer or incomplete
fusion channels in 6,7Li + 238U reactions
Homi Bhabha National Institute, Mumbai
Central University of Kerala, Kerala
The 238U target of thickness ~100 ug/cm2 was sandwiched
between two layers of 12C of thickness ~15 ug/cm2.
The fission fragments were detected by two
multiwire proportional counter (MWPC) detectors.
The active area of each detector was 16 X 11 cm2.
The central distances of MWPC1 and MWPC2 from the
target center were 39.5 and 33.5 cm, respectively.
For the time-of-flight measurements, the START of the
timing signals were taken from two transmission-type
gas detectors of active area 3.7 X 3.7 cm2 placed in
front of the MWPC detectors at a distance 11 cm from
the target center.
Two Si surface barrier detectors (M1 and M2)
were used as monitors.
Four CsI(Tl) detectors having four crystals
each were used to detect the projectile-like fragments
(PLFs).
No information on source of the uncertainty
A.Gandhi
